# CARE Library

> CARE Library is an open-source architecture for traceable, source-grounded, forkable and comparable interpretation of long-form texts.

CARE stands for Canonical Axiomatic Reduction Engine.

- Creator and project lead: William Proudler
- Current public version: v0.1.0-alpha
- Canonical website: https://carelibrary.org/
- Why CARE: https://carelibrary.org/why-care.html
- Public repository: https://github.com/willproudler/care-library
- Current flagship corpus: A Thousand Plateaus
- Current work: passage-grounding retrofit

Core principle: Interpretation cannot be made final, but it can be given coordinates.

Architecture: RAW → CH → CL → MC → WB → CB

Lateral operations include XS, XR and SC.
